Primary_Text: Tąy hǫǫteel niign nats'elsüüh. Tąy hǫǫteeł niign nats'elsüüh. nts'ą̈'  Meh nats'elmoo. Tąy hǫǫteeł niign nats'elsüü eh. Taylor Highway hǫǫsi'. Jah t'eey tąy teeł Taylor Highway mǫǫsi'. Ay ishyiit nts'änh noo nats'atdäl eh. T'axoh neeshyah Naambia Niign' hu'ąą dą' nts'ą̈' nats'atdäl eh. Dahdǫǫ shoh dat-sąy kah hach'edagn'įį'. Ay chih ts'eldeeł. Noołuut tah elk'ah, ushyi' nsǫǫ! Ay tl'aan dǫǫ nats'atdäl eh. Jah tąy k'üü noodlee iin nah'ogn tourist huhinay iin hushyah choh heetąą nts'ą̈'. Ch'itay <du'>, ch'ikąy' u'aat eh ishyiit nahideeh nts'ą̈' /picture/ shoh dat-sąy eh ehtsayh. Ha'atthiit shoh dat-sąy its'ą̈' na'ithät hatnagn'ąy eh. Roy naksä:::::ł. <<'Nih'įh?  Nih'įh?'>> 'Tthee!  Jah du' stop!' Roy t'oot'eey ah ay t'oot'eey hǫǫ't'eey ch'aałäł. Eh naksä:::l eh. T'axoh <nich'inįįłaat> ay tl'aan. K'at'eey k'at'eey <nich'ihniitl'aadn> iltsiil nts'ą̈' t'eey än' tinagntthät k'a' eh. Naxat dineh ch'ishyiign ehxaan shoh dat-sąy ay du' k'at'eey minaniich'akthädn nts'ą̈'. Shoh shoh nts'ą̈' agntthäl <eh>. Agntthäł nts'ą̈' uu'akdiih. Ay [laughs] dhakday eh hanuhshyign' tüh sdzina'altth'ät. Atthän' uu'agntthäł. Eh t'eey neeshye' Howard neek'eh t'eey niich'inįhłaat. Ay du': 'Dii?  Dii?  Dii ch'a dįh'ąy?' 'Shoh!' ``Dhakdayh hanuhshyign' tah dzina'altth'ät.' Nuhshyign' ik'eh teltthät ikooneh'įh. 'Nday?  Nday?'  nih, mookol ashyign' eh. Nushyign', tth'itu' nįįłąy ddhäł dą' nt'ay nushyign' tah eedah. Utthi' da' ne', shoh ch'a. Eh its'ą̈' telthät tl'aan nahiidhehxiin nuhshyign' tah. Ndüü:::::h! Ch'itay ch'a [laughs] ch'ishyiign eh ehxaan du': 'Shoh aldeeł?' 'Shoh aldeeł dii xah ch'a dįh'ąy?'  shehnih. 'Ąą, shiy ch'ale agndeel,' udihnih. K'at'eey minaniich'akthädn eh ay du' agnthäł nts'ą̈' t'eey uu'akdiih ch'ale. Ch'itay du' k'at'eey minaniich'akthädn. Shuudehka:::t. 'Nday?  Nday?' 'Ndee nushyign', tamaagn nts'ą̈' nushyign' tu tth'itu' nts'ą̈' tah shoh eedah.' 'Maa ni'uhshya' nts'ą̈' nok-'įį!'  nih. 'Ayt'oo, ayt'oo,' udihnih. Nushyign' tah ts'aahaal. Howard iin du' nushyign' tah hiik'anehta'. <Ay> ch'itay eh huxah ninįįshyah tl'aan nduugn nts'ą' natetshyah le' ishyiit nts'ą', k'at'eey udakkadn. Ayt'oo t'axoh.
Translated_Text: We hunt at the roadside. We hunt at the side of the highway. and Truck We hunt at the side of the highway. They call it Taylor Highway. Here on the road that is called Taylor Highway. And from there we were going back. Finally we were going back to our house in Northway. From uphill I was looking around for black bears. And also we eat them. In the fall they are fat, their meat is good! And then from way up there we were going back.  Here, next to the road, white people, the ones they call tourists, had parked their big camper. That old man, a husband and his wife were standing there and taking pictures of a black bear. Way down there I spotted a black bear standing, facing them. I hollered at Roy. "You see? "Wait! But Roy kept driving. And I was hollering. Finally he stopped and then. He didn't quite stop and I ran out with the gun. That man was taking pictures of the black bear, I didn't even think about him. I ran towards the bear. I'm shooting it while I'm running. I'm shooting and it tumbles down over the edge. I'm running down after it. And really our son Howard is parking behind us. He: "What? "Bear!" I was shooting and it tumbled down the slope. He ran down, following its tracks looking for it. "Where? Way down, where the river is flowing, down the mountain, it was sitting. It was turning its head in different directions, the bear. And he was running to it and then they killed it down there. It was heavy! That old man who was taking pictures [said]: "Do you eat bear?" "Are you messing with it because you eat bear?" "Yes, as for me, I eat them," I told him. I didn't even think about it; I was running and then I was shooting it. I wasn't thinking about that old man though. He kept asking me questions. "Where? "Way down there, at the water's edge, by the river, the bear is sitting." "I want to go there and see it!" "It's alright, it's alright," I tell him. The two of us are walking down there. Howard and the others down there are taking care of it. And that old man is walking up to them and I don't know where he went from there, I didn't ask. That's it.
